challenges.title: Content-First Breakpoints
challenges.description: Take a single-column layout and add exactly 3 content-based breakpoints where the layout naturally needs them.
challenges.instructions: Create a page with text, images, and cards. Add breakpoints only where content looks awkward. Name your breakpoints based on the content change.
hints: Resize slowly and watch for text line-length issues
Look for when cards have too much whitespace
Add a breakpoint when navigation items have room to be horizontal

challenges.title: Custom Framework Breakpoints
challenges.description: Design a custom breakpoint system with Sass/CSS variables and use it consistently across components.
challenges.instructions: Define 5 breakpoints as CSS custom properties. Create reusable utility classes that use these breakpoints.
hints: Use CSS custom properties for breakpoint values

challenges.title: Fluid Layout with Minimum Breakpoints
challenges.description: Build a complete page layout with a 1-column, 2-column, and 3-column grid using only 2 breakpoints. Use CSS Grid auto-fit for the rest.
challenges.instructions: Combine auto-fit grids with strategic breakpoints to create a layout that works at all widths with minimal media queries.
hints: Use auto-fit with minmax for the main column flexibility
Use explicit breakpoints only when grid-template-columns needs to change fundamentally
Consider using clamp() for fluid sizing between breakpoints
